system: POI-POWER — Points of Power + the Item Economy for rapp-go. Real OSM places, queried live via Overpass, become spinnable "wells" that give tactile, natural items on a per-place replenish cooldown; those items are the currency the catch/encounter systems spend to keep a moment.
design:
CORE LOOP: real place → spin when in range → deterministic drop of natural items → per-POI cooldown → items feed the catch system → catching frees bag space → walk to the next place. No server; replenishment is just elapsed wall-clock.

POI MODEL (normalized from an OSM element, stable id):
  { id:"node/123"|"way/789", lat, lng, name, kind, tags:{name,amenity,...} }
KIND is classified from tags into 6 soul-carrying flavors (drives the drop table):
  water   (amenity=drinking_water|fountain, natural=spring)        → life/offerings
  nature  (natural=tree|peak|rock, leisure=park|garden)            → offerings + lures
  landmark(tourism=artwork|attraction|viewpoint|museum, historic=*, memorial) → rare vessels + plates
  worship (amenity=place_of_worship)                               → rare, incense
  civic   (amenity=library|townhall|marketplace|clock, tourism=information) → mixed
  seat    (amenity=bench + anything unmatched)                     → thin, common only

ITEM SET (7 items — natural, tactile, never "arcade"):
  Vessels of light (the catch orbs, 3 tiers) — thrown to hold a creature, CONSUMED on throw:
    vessel.glass  tier0  catchMult 1.00   (common workhorse)
    vessel.dew    tier1  catchMult 1.40   (uncommon)
    vessel.prism  tier2  catchMult 1.90   (rare; splits the light — best hold)
  Offerings (aids spent during an encounter):
    offering.salt   calm: fleeMult 0.50, catchMult 1.15  (common, lasts the encounter)
    offering.honey  fleeMult 0.60, catchMult 1.90, single-throw  (rare golden aid)
  Tools:
    lure   — consumed to "quicken" a POI: forces a place+now spawn near it for 20 min (shared-place flavor)
    plate  — a photographic plate; consumed to "develop" a rapp-snap photo into a catchable encounter (gates the image-capture path lightly)
ITEMS[id] = { id, name, glyph, kind:'vessel'|'offering'|'tool', tier?, catchMult?, fleeMult?, oneThrow?, desc } — the catch RNG reads catchMult/fleeMult straight off this; UI reads name/glyph.

DROP TABLES (weighted; per-POI-kind, plus a draw count so places have generosity character):
  water    draws 4 : glass 55, salt 30, dew 12, honey 3
  nature   draws 4 : glass 45, dew 20, salt 20, lure 5, honey 5, plate 5
  landmark draws 3 : dew 30, glass 30, prism 12, plate 15, honey 8, lure 5
  worship  draws 3 : dew 34, glass 26, prism 14, honey 12, lure 8, plate 6
  civic    draws 4 : glass 50, dew 25, salt 15, lure 5, plate 5
  seat     draws 2 : glass 70, salt 25, dew 5
prism (best vessel) only appears at landmark/worship → you must walk to monuments to get strong holds. Small (~8%) chance of one bonus draw. A drop of a vessel/offering is count 1; a rare tool is count 1.

DETERMINISM (where it should be): a spin's drops are deterministic from mkRng(poi.id + ':' + spinCount). spinCount is persisted and only increments on a COMMITTED spin, so a place has a reproducible "character," results can't be silently re-rolled by refresh, yet each visit differs. Catch success stays live RNG (the sibling's), lure spawns stay deterministic-from-place+time via the existing open-meteo→momentToGenome pipeline.

RANGE + COOLDOWN: spin allowed only when haversine(player, poi) ≤ SPIN_RADIUS (40 m, + GPS-accuracy slack). After a spin the POI is spent for COOLDOWN_MS (default 5 min); ready again when now−lastSpin ≥ COOLDOWN. Map ring = glowing(ready)/dim(spent, with a slow refill arc). SCARCITY: soft bag cap (BAG=350 total items) creates pressure to SPEND vessels by catching rather than hoard — catching is what makes room, closing the gather↔catch loop.

PERSISTENCE (client-only):
  localStorage 'rapp-go.inv'   = { itemId: count }
  localStorage 'rapp-go.poi'   = { poiId: { t:lastSpinEpoch, n:spinCount } }  (pruned to last ~500)
  localStorage 'rapp-go.lures' = { poiId: expiryEpoch }
  IndexedDB 'rapp-go' store 'tiles' keyed tileId → { tileId, fetchedAt, pois:[...], attribution } (larger payloads)

PUBLIC API (the contract siblings build on — one ES module, no build step):
  refreshPOIs(lat,lng) → POI[]        // ensures tiles around player cached, returns merged POIs
  poisInView(bbox) → POI[]
  poiStatus(poi, playerLatLng) → { inRange, ready, readyInMs, spinCount, lured }
  spinPOI(poi, playerLatLng) → { ok, drops:[{item,count}], error }  // enforces range+cooldown, credits inv, bumps spinCount, sets cooldown
  getInventory() → { itemId:count } ; spendItem(id,n=1)→bool ; bagCount()→int
  placeLure(poi)→bool ; activeLures()→[{poi,expiresAt}]
  ITEMS  (the definition table above)
The catch system calls spendItem('vessel.dew') on a throw and reads ITEMS[id].catchMult/fleeMult for its formula; the bag UI reads getInventory()+ITEMS.
integration:
Feeds the existing ONE gene pool, doesn't fork it:
- Vessels/offerings gathered here are the currency the CATCH system (sibling) spends; it calls spendItem() and reads ITEMS[id].catchMult/fleeMult. On a successful catch the encounter system builds the egg with the SAME plumbing already in hologram/index.html — momentToGenome({temp,weathercode,wind,isDay}) from the POI's live open-meteo current (same URL as L1087), genomeId() for the content-addressed id, cart shape {schema:'hologram-cartridge/1.0',id,title,author,born:{coord,from},parents:[],genome,sig:''}. born.coord = geohashEncode(poi.lat,poi.lng)+'·'+Date.now() so the keepsake carries the REAL place+moment, and I add home:{poi:poi.id, name:poi.name} so the creature remembers the named place it was caught at (home already flows through breed/lineage untouched — see L747).
- LURE integration: placeLure(poi) hands the encounter system a spawn descriptor {lat:poi.lat,lng:poi.lng,poiId,bornAt:Date.now()} → it runs the exact open-meteo→momentToGenome pipeline to birth a place-true creature. PLATE gates the rapp-snap path (photo→palette→genome) into a catchable encounter.
- Caught eggs land in the shared 'rapp-basket'/'eggs' IndexedDB store via the identical keepToBasket() record shape (id,egg,title,born,addedAt) → they instantly appear in the CABINET's kept strip (loadKeptStrip, L445), can be BRED (crossBreed), TALKED to in the COMPANION (inject egg), and opened in the LANTERN via cartPlayerHref/#-token. Deep-link handoff reuses #adopt=<b64enc(JSON)> into the cabinet gene pool (L648).
- rapp-go uses a SEPARATE IndexedDB 'rapp-go' for tiles and localStorage 'rapp-go.*' for inventory/cooldowns so it never bumps the basket DB version (basket is v1/eggs-only; a version bump would race the cabinet). Geohash/mkRng are copied verbatim so both surfaces stay byte-identical on the shared genome id math.
ontaste: The stops are REAL named places — "Cedar Street Fountain," "War Memorial," a specific oak — not neon towers; the label is the place's own OSM name. What a place gives fits its nature: water gives offerings (life/salt/honey), monuments give the rare prism vessels, a bench gives a little. The items are matter, not merch — glass, dew, prism, salt, honey — no "Ultra Balls," no tiers screaming rarity. The spin is a slow dimming ring and a quiet refill arc, not a slot machine. Cooldown is patience, not a countdown you're nagged about. Attribution to OpenStreetMap contributors sits visibly on every place card — a quiet act of respect for the real map you're walking. The whole economy exists only to make the moment worth keeping: you gather a little, you spend it to hold one real sky, and the place gives again when you come back.
risks: Overpass rate limits / 429 / downtime: mitigate with 14-day tile cache, ≥8 s throttle, fetch only on tile-miss, endpoint failover to the kumi mirror, exponential backoff, and graceful degrade to cached/incense-only mode — never spam the API.

Sparse/rural POIs: widen the bbox margin, and provide a fallback 'your own location is a faint well' (low-yield, long-cooldown, glass+salt only) so the loop never dead-ends; lure/incense let a player self-spawn where there are no stops.
OSM/ODbL attribution is mandatory: '© OpenStreetMap contributors' persisted per tile and shown on every place card and the map — build-time non-negotiable.

Coarse-location privacy: querying by geohash-6 tile-center bbox (not the exact GPS point) both cuts request rate and avoids handing Overpass the player's precise position; geolocation itself never leaves the device.

GPS drift / spoofing to spin out of range: range check with accuracy slack (min(pos.accuracy,25 m)); can't fully stop a determined spoofer, but it's single-player and self-only so the only person cheated is the player.

localStorage growth + clearing: prune poi-state to the last ~500 entries; document that clearing storage resets inventory/cooldowns (spinCount reset only re-flavors future spins) — but eggs are safe in the separate basket DB.
Battery/geolocation drain: use watchPosition throttled to ~1 Hz, pause the watch and Overpass on document.hidden, debounce tile checks.
Bag-cap frustration: BAG=350 soft cap with a gentle 'bag full' note on overflow, and it's intentional pressure to catch (spend) rather than hoard — surface it as design, not error.

CORS on mirrors: overpass-api.de and overpass.kumi.systems both send permissive CORS today; if a mirror drops headers the failover list and cache absorb it, and the module degrades rather than throws.
mvp: Smallest genuinely playable slice (no map, no AR, no lures/plate needed): rapp-go/index.html + items.js(vessel.glass, vessel.dew, offering.salt only) + overpass.js + store.js + geo.js + rng.js + poi-power.js. Flow: get geolocation → refreshPOIs() does ONE Overpass fetch for the current geohash-6 tile (cached in IndexedDB) → render a distance-sorted 'nearby' list, each row showing name, kind glyph, distance, and a ready/spent state → when a row is within 40 m the Spin button enables → spinPOI() rolls the deterministic drop, credits the localStorage inventory, and starts the 5-min cooldown (row goes dim with a refill) → a bag drawer shows getInventory(). That alone is 'gotta catch the weather' economics working end-to-end and hardened for the API's policy. spendItem() is already exported so the moment the sibling catch system lands, these vessels immediately do their job. Add lure/plate/prism, the raster map, and the ready/refill ring in the next pass.
